title: Austin, Texas
summary: Two pilgrims in Austin, Texas. Wind and warmth. Three and a half kilometers along a lake. They talked about memory and forgetting. About ego and flow. About circles that spiral upward. Some of the most important things will never teach. They can only be walked.

title: Austin, Texas
summary: A pilgrim in Austin, Texas. Overcast sky. Thirty degrees. Four kilometers along a lake. They had a story to tell. Three words became an idea. The idea became an app. The app became this. No one asked them to build it. They just did. That is already more than most people managed today.
transcript:
Hello world, that's what you say as a programmer, trying to make an app for the first time, and that's the first message that you give out to the world. So it's appropriate, I guess, for this podcast to say hello world, the pilgrim on the path. So the idea of this is when people are doing a walk, they can talk, and the talk presumably if it fits the right amount of talk time, there's an eligibility for you to submit your talk which will get curated and be part of the Pilgrim on the path podcast which will be on a separate page called podcast. Pilgrimapp.org where you can see all of the podcasts which also gets distributed to various platforms like Apple Spotify. And there's no goal, just pilgrims, anonymously submitting their talks about life, about general anything. It could be literally anything, and just various walks of life anonymously. It doesn't need to know who you are. They're just getting to know someone out there in the world about this. The first episode I feel like there's a great place to introduce why program, why the app, how to came about, what was the inspiration and why I feel other people might want this, which basically I built it for myself and see how it changes people. I feel like there's a great change right now it's already happening, but also just planetary shifts, astrologically there's big happening. I see around and a lot of people's lives currently and it's happening. So I feel like it's a great time to reflect, to walk and talk. Also meditate. And we kept in touch and chatted and he was asking about how I am working towards that.
